question: Does the image layout consist of three equally wide panels arranged horizontally in the upper section and a full-width table in the bottom section?
In Panel A (left), are the principal optical axis and vertical lens symbol drawn, with focal points (F1, F2) and 2F points (2F1, 2F2) marked symmetrically?
In Panel A, is the object arrow (OBJ) pointing upward and positioned on the left side of the lens?
In Panel A, are three distinct principal rays drawn (parallel-to-focus, through-center, focus-to-parallel) that converge to form the image?
In Panel A, is the image arrow (IMG) inverted, positioned at the ray intersection, and connected with dashed auxiliary lines?
In Panel B (middle), is a concave mirror arc drawn on the right side, with the Center of curvature (C) and Focal point (F) marked on the axis?
In Panel B, is the object arrow (OBJ) positioned visually between the focal point (F) and the center of curvature (C)?
In Panel B, is the resulting image (IMG) depicted as inverted, magnified, and located beyond the center of curvature (C)?
In Panel B, are the normal line and incident/reflection angle arcs labeled 'A1' and 'A2' visible?
Does Panel C (right) depict an equilateral triangular prism?
In Panel C, does a single ray enter from the left, travel as a straight line segment inside the prism, and split into three rays upon exiting?
In Panel C, do the exiting rays consist of distinct Red, Green, and Blue lines fanning out in that specific order?
In Panel C, are normal lines drawn at the entry and exit points, with angle arcs labeled I1, R1, I2, and R2?
Does the bottom parameter table have exactly 4 columns and headers labeled 'ITEM', 'SYMBOL', 'VALUE', 'UNIT'?
Does the table contain the data row: 'AIR', symbol 'n', value '1.00'?
Does the table contain the data row: 'GLASS', symbol 'n', value '1.50'?
Does the table contain the data row: 'LENS', symbol 'f', value '50', and unit 'mm'?
Is the image rendered as vector line art on a white background, free of shadows and gradients?
Is the color scheme primarily black/gray, with necessary light rays rendered in blue or RGB colors?
Are all text labels (e.g., F1, OBJ, table values) clearly readable and free of garbled characters?
